title: Mixed Graph: The charts below give information about a public library in a town called Little...
description: The charts below give information about a public library in a town called Little Chalfont. Summarise the information by selecting and report
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Mixed charts: Library (pie \+ table \+ bar)

1. Giải thích đề

Biểu đồ tròn: độ tuổi thành viên thư viện (2016).

Bảng: tỷ lệ sách/mượn theo thể loại (2016).

Biểu đồ cột: tổng số lượt mượn từ 2007–2016.

→ Dạng: mixed nhiều biểu đồ (overview tổng \+ chi tiết)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

age of members

total loans by category

total number of loans

Mẫu:

The charts provide information about the age distribution of library members, the proportion of loans by category in 2016, and the total number of loans between 2007 and 2016.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Người ≥65 tuổi chiếm phần lớn

Fiction (trẻ em & người lớn) là thể loại mượn nhiều nhất

Tổng số lượt mượn nhìn chung tăng theo thời gian, dù có dao động nhẹ

4. BODY 1 – Pie chart (age)

Adults 65+: 51% (lớn nhất)

Young adults (13–17): 22%

Adults (18–64): 15%

Children: 12%

→ Ý chính:

  • Người cao tuổi chiếm hơn một nửa
  • Trẻ em là nhóm nhỏ nhất

5. BODY 2 – Table (loan categories)

Children’s fiction: 38%

Adult fiction: 38% (đồng cao nhất)

Adult non-fiction: 13%

Children’s non-fiction: 6%

Young adults: 2%

Adult audiobooks: 2%

Children’s DVDs: 1% (thấp nhất)

→ Ý chính:

  • Fiction dominate
  • Các loại còn lại chiếm tỷ lệ nhỏ

6. BODY 3 – Bar chart (total loans)

2007: \~15,500

2008–2011: tăng dần (\~19k → \~21k)

2012–2013: giảm nhẹ (\~20k → \~19.5k)

2014–2016: tăng lại, đạt \~22,000 (cao nhất)

→ Ý chính:

  • Xu hướng chung là tăng
  • Có một giai đoạn giảm nhẹ giữa kỳ

The charts provide information about the age distribution of members at Little Chalfont Library in 2016, the breakdown of loans by category in the same year, and the total number of loans from 2007 to 2016.

People aged 65 and over made up the largest proportion of members, accounting for 51%. Young adults (13–17) represented 22%, while adults aged 18–64 comprised 15%. Children formed the smallest group at 12%.

In terms of borrowing, fiction dominated. Both children’s fiction and adult fiction each accounted for 38% of total loans, making them the most popular categories. Adult non-fiction contributed 13%, whereas children’s non-fiction made up 6%. The remaining categories were relatively minor, with young adults at 2%, adult audiobooks at 2%, and children’s DVDs at just 1%.

Looking at trends over time, the total number of loans generally increased despite some fluctuations. Loans rose from around 15,000 in 2007 to approximately 21,000 in 2011, before dipping slightly to just under 20,000 in 2013. After that, the figure recovered and continued to climb, reaching a peak of about 22,000 in 2016.
